---
pagetitle: "Frameworks for Public Policy"
page-layout: full
toc: false
listing:
  - id: featured
    contents:
      - public-policy/wicked-problems.qmd
      - public-policy/ooo.qmd
      - political-thinking/overton-window.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 3
    fields: [image, title, summary, categories]
    image-height: "200px"
    sort: false
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.tagline}
A growing collection of mental models for thinking about policy, drawn from books, papers, and 300+ editions of *Anticipating the Unintended*. Frameworks-based thinking helps make sense of complex policy problems and what can be done about them.
:::

::: {.sub-tagline}
Curated and explained by [Pranay Kotasthane](https://pranaykotas.com).
:::
:::

## Find a framework

Describe the policy problem you're thinking about and get the most relevant frameworks.

::: {.finder-box}

::: {.finder-input-row}
<input type="text" id="finder-input" class="finder-input" placeholder="e.g. why infrastructure projects fail, how reforms become politically acceptable…" />
<button id="finder-btn" class="finder-btn">Find</button>
:::

::: {.example-chips}
<button class="example-chip" data-query="why infrastructure projects fail">infrastructure failures</button>
<button class="example-chip" data-query="how narrative shifts policy acceptability">narrative and policy</button>
<button class="example-chip" data-query="matching policy instruments to objectives">instrument selection</button>
<button class="example-chip" data-query="deterrence and state behaviour">deterrence</button>
<button class="example-chip" data-query="fiscal federalism and taxation">fiscal federalism</button>
:::

<div id="finder-results"></div>

:::

<script src="scripts/finder.js"></script>

## Start here

Three frameworks that show the range of what's on this site — analysis, evaluation, politics.

::: {#featured}
:::

## Browse by category

::: {.category-grid}

[**Public Policy**<br>
[Frameworks for defining policy problems, analysing failures, and matching tools to objectives.]{.category-tile-desc}](public-policy/index.qmd){.category-tile}

[**Political Thinking**<br>
[How political coalitions form, narratives travel, and ideas become acceptable.]{.category-tile-desc}](political-thinking/index.qmd){.category-tile}

[**Public Finance**<br>
[State functions, debt sustainability, taxation, and fiscal federalism.]{.category-tile-desc}](public-finance/index.qmd){.category-tile}

[**Foreign Policy & Geopolitics**<br>
[State behaviour, deterrence, world orders, and India's strategic choices.]{.category-tile-desc}](foreign-policy-defence-geopolitics/index.qmd){.category-tile}

[**Society**<br>
[Social norms, networked societies, ideology, and information ecosystems.]{.category-tile-desc}](society/index.qmd){.category-tile}

[**Universe**<br>
[Modelling, trade-offs, and frameworks that cut across domains.]{.category-tile-desc}](universe/index.qmd){.category-tile}

:::
